observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management for the immediate physical inventory taking and reporting and require the accountant to reconcile the balances appearing in the report with that of the concerned officer and effect the necessary adjustment after establishing the correctness of the balance of the PPE account.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the DRRM officer and other concerned officials to support all claims with sufficient and required documentations, particularly, on the utilization of the COVID 19 appropriated funds in their procurement of medical related goods and services. Likewise, management must strictly observe laws, rules and regulations as provided for in Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on proper and sufficient required documentations and Section 52.1 of RA 9184 on Shopping as an alternative mode of procurement.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.

We have recommended that the group tasked to function as the municipality’s LDRRMO be required to prepare a report on the utilization of LDRRMF to be submitted in accordance with the provisions of paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Further, QRF should be transferred immediately from GF to TF books at the end of the year.

We have recommended that accountable officers must strictly adhere to the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any officials or employees unless the previous cash advance is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made. Furthermore, liquidation of cash advances should be made as required by regulation.
We recommended that the management to support all claims with the required documentations prior to release of payments / checks to validate disbursements.

We recommend that management observe the general guideline stipulated in COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies. This is to promote good governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a family.

We recommended to the management for the immediate physical inventory taking
and reporting and require the accountant to reconcile the balances appearing in
the report with that of the concerned officer and effect the necessary adjustment
after establishing the correctness of the balance of the PPE account.

We recommended that Municipal Engineer must submit immediately the
necessary documents for inspection of various Infrastructure project by the COA
Engineers from Technical and Information Technology Services. Likewise the MPDC
should strictly follow the guidelines stated in the DILG and DBM Circular in programming
the 20% Local Development Fund.

We recommend that management should direct the procuring department to notify
the Audit Team Leader or any of its representatives on any procurement of
supplies, materials and office equipment consummated for the agency’s
operational purposes during the procuring period within 24 hours after acceptance thereof.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the DRRM officer and other
concerned officials to support all claims with sufficient and required
documentations, particularly, on the utilization of the COVID 19 appropriated funds
in their procurement of medical related goods and services. Likewise, management
must strictly observe laws, rules and regulations as provided for in Section 4(6) of
PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on proper and sufficient
required documentations and Section 52.1 of RA 9184 on Shopping as an alternative
mode of procurement.

We recommend the concerned accountable officers particularly the Provincial
Treasurer and the Provincial Accountant to strictly observe the schedule for rendition
of accounts as required for under Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 otherwise known
as the “State Audit Code of the Philippines” and likewise Section 6.03 of COA Cir.
No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommend that proper internal control over supplies or property be adopted
and it demands that, proper and accurate recording as to its valuation, existence,
ownership and completeness shall be kept for all incoming and out-going supplies or
property in pursuant to Sec. 42, 45 to 46 of NGAS Manual Vol. I

We recommend that management observe the general guideline stipulated in COA
Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of
Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies. This is to promote good
governance through transparency and accountability.

We have recommended that the group tasked to function as the municipality’s
LDRRMO be required to prepare a report on the utilization of LDRRMF to be
submitted in accordance with the provisions of paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No.
2012-002. Further, QRF should be transferred immediately from GF to TF books at
the end of the year.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change
management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste
Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement
for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to
facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to
establish accountability of the responsible person.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of law as cited
above by remitting promptly LGUs share and employee’s personal contributions and
deduction in order not to hamper the benefits that the employee may receive and to
avoid penalty and legal sanctions and past due accounts like this should be given
priority to determine the correct unremitted balances, so that, said account will no
longer be carried over and over again in the financial statement.

We have recommended that accountable officers must strictly adhere to the COA guidelines
that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any officials or employees unless the
previous cash advance is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made. Furthermore,
liquidation of cash advances should be made as required by regulation.

We recommended that the management to support all claims with the required
documentations prior to release of payments / checks to validate disbursements.

Management should require the municipal accountant and municipal treasurer to
observe strictly the schedule of rendition of accounts, in accordance with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006, so that the Auditor could perform timely
audit of the financial transactions of the agency.

We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the proper
disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation of
the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy programs
promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender based
violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a family.
We recommended that MPDC should strictly follow the guidelines stated in the DILG and DBM Circular in programming the 20% Local Development Fund.

We recommend that the management direct the concerned accountant to observe the perpetual system in recording purchases and to use the RSMI in recording issuances and consumption of stocks and prepare adjusting entries for all noted errors in recording to correct the balances of inventory accounts.

We recommend that proper internal control over supplies or property be adopted and it demands that, proper and accurate recording as to its valuation, existence, ownership and completeness shall be kept for all incoming and out-going supplies or property in pursuant to Sec. 42, 45 to 46 of NGAS Manual Vol. I

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of law as cited above by remitting promptly the employee’s personal contributions and deduction in order not to hamper the benefits that the employee may receive and to avoid penalty and legal sanctions and past due accounts like this should be given priority to determine the correct unremitted balances, so that, said account will no longer be carried over and over again in the financial statement.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.

We recommend that concerned officials should fast tract the implementation of PPAs covered by these fund transfers in order to accomplish the purpose for which the funds were granted and deliver immediate and optimum result to target beneficiaries; and require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the liquidation reports to the source agency.

We recommended to the management for the immediate  physical  inventory
     taking and reporting  and  require  the  accountant  to  reconcile  the
     balances appearing in the report with that of the concerned officer and
     effect the necessary adjustment after establishing the  correctness  of
     the balance of the PPE account.

We recommended that Municipal Engineer must request for  inspection  of
   various Infrastructure projects and enhance cooperation  and  support  to
   the COA Inspectorate Team from the  Regional  Technical  and  Information
   Technology Services in the inspection of said  project  by  providing  it
   with the complete project requirements and guiding the COA  engineers  to
   the  project  sites.   Likewise  the  MPDC  should  strictly  follow  the
   guidelines stated in the DILG and DBM Circular  in  programming  the  20%
   Local Development Fund.

We  have  recommended  that  the  group  tasked  to  function   as   the
   municipality’s LDRRMO be required to prepare a report on the utilization
   of LDRRMF to be submitted in accordance with the provisions of paragraph
   5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Further, QRF should  be  transferred
   immediately from GF to TF books at the end of the year.

We have recommended that accountable officers  must  strictly  adhere
   to the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance shall  be  allowed
   to any officials or employees unless the previous cash advance is  first
   settled or proper accounting thereof is made.  Furthermore,  liquidation
   of cash advances should be made as required by regulation.

We recommended that the  management  to  support  all  claims  with  the
    required documentations  prior  to  release  of  payments  /  checks  to
    validate disbursements.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the  provisions  of
   law as  cited  above  by  remitting  promptly  the  employee’s  personal
   contributions and deduction in order not to hamper the benefits that the
   employee may receive and to avoid penalty and legal sanctions  and  past
   due accounts like this should be given priority to determine the correct
   unremitted balances, so that, said account will  no  longer  be  carried
   over and over again in the financial statement.

We recommend that management observe the general guideline  stipulated
   in COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January  30,  2013  RE:  Information  and
   Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of  government  agencies.
   This  is  to  promote   good   governance   through   transparency   and
   accountability.

The GAD implementation Report should be prepared quarterly by  the  team
   for proper monitoring and  evaluation  of  accomplishments  as  well  as
   identifying the major constraints or problems  encountered,  and  submit
   the same to the office of the audit team leader.


   We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure  the
   proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate  and  review  the
   implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender  and  Development,
   conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men  and  women,
   discouraging gender  based  violence  between  them  and  encouraging  a
   greater role for men in raising a family.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management  be  formalize  and  change
   management strategy in the implementation  of  a  realistic  and  doable
   Solid Waste Management Plan.   Develop  an  Action/Implementation  Plan.
   Draw an arrangement for the  Plan  and  identify  possible  risks  in  a
   comprehensive manner  in  order  to  facilitate  proper  implementation,
   monitoring and measurement of results, and to  establish  accountability
   of the responsible person.

discouraging
gender based
violence between
them and
encouraging a
greater role for
men in raising a
family.
observations.managementAction:
Mgt.
assured the
Auditor to
comply with
the audit
finding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management for the immediate  physical  inventory
     taking and reporting  and  require  the  accountant  to  reconcile  the
     balances appearing in the report with that of the concerned officer and
     effect the necessary adjustment after establishing the  correctness  of
     the balance of the PPE account.

We recommended that Municipal Engineer must request for  inspection  of
   various Infrastructure projects and enhance cooperation  and  support  to
   the COA Inspectorate Team from the  Regional  Technical  and  Information
   Technology Services in the inspection of said  project  by  providing  it
   with the complete project requirements and guiding the COA  engineers  to
   the  project  sites.   Likewise  the  MPDC  should  strictly  follow  the
   guidelines stated in the DILG and DBM Circular  in  programming  the  20%
   Local Development Fund.

We have recommended that accountable officers  must  strictly  adhere
   to the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance shall  be  allowed
   to any officials or employees unless the previous cash advance is  first
   settled or proper accounting thereof is made.  Furthermore,  liquidation
   of cash advances should be made as required by regulation.

We recommended that the  management  to  support  all  claims  with  the
    required documentations  prior  to  release  of  payments  /  checks  to
    validate disbursements.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management  be  formalize  and  change
   management strategy in the implementation  of  a  realistic  and  doable
   Solid Waste Management Plan.   Develop  an  Action/Implementation  Plan.
   Draw an arrangement for the  Plan  and  identify  possible  risks  in  a
   comprehensive manner  in  order  to  facilitate  proper  implementation,
   monitoring and measurement of results, and to  establish  accountability
   of the responsible person.

We  recommend  that  concerned  officials  should   fast   tract   the
     implementation of PPAs covered by these fund  transfers  in  order  to
     accomplish the purpose for which the funds were  granted  and  deliver
     immediate and optimum result to target beneficiaries; and require  the
     Municipal Accountant to facilitate  the  liquidation  reports  to  the
     source agency.

We recommended
that cash
advances shall be
liquidated
immediately and
refrain from
obtaining cash
advances for
purposes other
than travelling
expenses. Payment
of operating
expenses either
for
administrative or
infrastructure
purposes should
be made directly
to the
supplier/contract
or and not
through cash
advance nor
reimbursement
except for
reimbursements
shall be allowed.

must conduct
aggressive
collection
campaign to
maximize local
revenue.   They
must implement
Section 246, 247,
248, 249, 250 and
251, of the Local
Government Code
in order to
enhance tax
collection to
hasten economic
development.

ion.
